instruction:
We now define an algorithm: The definition of a(n) is: Triangle in which first row is 0, n-th row (n>1) lists the exponents of distinct prime factors ("ordered prime signature") in the prime factorization of n. For example, the prime factorization of 12 is 2^2 * 3^1, so it contributes the row [2, 1]. Given the input x_list (a series of values): [111, 112, 113, 114, 115, 116, 117, 118, 119, 120], determine the corresponding output sequence y_list.

Options:
A. [1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 3, 2, 1]
B. [1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 3, 1, 1, 1, 2]
C. [1, 1, 2, 1, 3, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1]
D. [1, 1, 3, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 2]
E. [1, 2, 1, 1, 3, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1]
F. [3, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 3]
G. [2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 3, 1]
H. [1, 2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 2, 3, 1, 1]
I. [2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 3, 2]
J. [1, 1, 1, 2, 1, 1, 3, 1, 1, 2]
